task_completion.visual_evidence: The AstroImageJ main toolbar or application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the sequence of 25 FITS images from the quality_control directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window in AstroImageJ is open displaying a FITS image, with a scrollbar or stack indicator at the bottom showing a sequence of 25 images (e.g., '1/25').

task_completion.description: Visually inspect the image sequence to find degraded frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the image stack slider in AstroImageJ, viewing frames that exhibit visible degradation such as blur, wind shake (double stars), or transparency loss (dimness).

task_completion.description: Open a text editor to record the identified bad frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor application (e.g., Mousepad, Gedit, or a terminal-based editor like nano) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: List the 3 degraded frames in bad_frames.txt on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor contains exactly 'frame_006.fits', 'frame_014.fits', and 'frame_021.fits' on separate lines. The file must be saved as 'bad_frames.txt' on the Desktop, which can be verified via the editor's title bar, save dialog, or a file manager window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used AstroImageJ to inspect the images rather than using terminal scripts to cheat.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the AstroImageJ GUI being used to view the FITS files. The agent should not be using terminal commands (like Python scripts or ls -l) to detect file size or metadata differences as a shortcut.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually viewed the images before writing the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The AstroImageJ image stack must be opened and interacted with before the bad_frames.txt file is populated with the correct filenames.

integrity.description: Verify the output file is genuinely saved to the Desktop.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor's save path or a terminal/file manager confirms the file is located at ~/Desktop/bad_frames.txt, not just held in an unsaved buffer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 25 frames. The 3 artificially degraded frames that must be identified are exactly: frame_006.fits (transparency drop), frame_014.fits (wind shake), and frame_021.fits (defocus blur). The output file must be located at /home/ga/Desktop/bad_frames.txt and contain exactly these three filenames, one per line.
